title: LA Recording Studio Finder
shortOutcome: A production directory that turns a fragmented studio search into a location-aware, comparable decision.
role: Product designer, developer, researcher, and operator
ownership: Original directory product, data workflow, search experience, content system, deployment, and production operations. Studio facts originate from public business information and are maintained as directory data.

evidence.label: Production
evidence.detail: The directory is deployed on Vercel with a custom production domain.

evidence.label: Discovery model
evidence.detail: Visitors can compare studios through geographic and structured directory context.

evidence.label: Search foundation
evidence.detail: Canonical routes, sitemap controls, thin-page audits, and content standards support organic discovery.

problem.title: Finding a studio is a local-data problem disguised as a search box.
body: Artists often compare studios across scattered social profiles, map listings, outdated websites, and word of mouth. Important differences—location, room type, equipment, services, and fit—are difficult to evaluate consistently.
The directory creates one browsable decision surface while giving individual studios durable, search-friendly pages.

ownership.title: A solo product spanning interface, data, and production operations.
body: I designed and implemented the product, built its studio data and update workflow, created the map and discovery experience, established content and SEO standards, and operate the Vercel deployment.
Directory listings summarize public business information. A listing is not an endorsement, and accuracy depends on an ongoing maintenance loop.

systemMap.label: Studio sources
systemMap.detail: Public business sites, location data, services, rooms, and amenities.

systemMap.label: Directory records
systemMap.detail: Normalized studio fields and editorial context form a maintained dataset.

systemMap.label: Quality checks
systemMap.detail: Required fields, content depth, route integrity, and duplicate review.

systemMap.label: Discovery UI
systemMap.detail: Search, filters, map context, recommendations, and detail pages.

systemMap.label: Search surface
systemMap.detail: Canonical metadata, sitemaps, index controls, and content audits.

systemMap.label: Correction loop
systemMap.detail: Stale facts, closed studios, and thin records return to review.

decisions.title: Directory usefulness before directory size
decisions.decision: Treat content depth and comparison value as quality gates rather than maximizing listing count.
decisions.consequence: The product is designed around making a decision, not merely generating indexable pages.

decisions.title: Geography is part of the interface
decisions.decision: Combine structured filters with map context instead of forcing either list-only or map-only browsing.
decisions.consequence: Visitors can reason about both studio fit and the practical cost of getting there.

decisions.title: SEO rules are product rules
decisions.decision: Canonicalization, parameter handling, sitemap inclusion, and thin-content checks are explicit production gates.
decisions.consequence: Search acquisition is maintained as a testable system rather than a one-time metadata pass.

buildRecord.label: 01 / MODEL
buildRecord.title: Comparable studio records
buildRecord.detail: Unstructured public information becomes consistent fields and editorial summaries.

buildRecord.label: 02 / DISCOVER
buildRecord.title: Map and directory experience
buildRecord.detail: Visitors move between geographic context, filters, recommendations, and studio detail.

buildRecord.label: 03 / PUBLISH
buildRecord.title: Search-ready route system
buildRecord.detail: Canonical pages, sitemap policy, metadata, and parameter controls support discoverability.

buildRecord.label: 04 / OPERATE
buildRecord.title: Production audit loop
buildRecord.detail: Scripts and review procedures detect thin, JS-dependent, stale, and incorrectly indexed pages.

failures.title: More pages did not automatically create more value
failures.observed: Thin or repetitive directory pages risked weak visitor utility and weak search performance.
failures.response: Introduced content-depth checks, public-route probes, and an audit that flags thin indexable pages.

failures.title: Filter URLs can pollute the search index
failures.observed: Tracking and query variants can create duplicate or low-value route combinations.
failures.response: Canonicalize filter surfaces, redirect tracking parameters, and keep the sitemap restricted to intended public pages.

failures.title: Directory facts age
failures.observed: Rates, rooms, equipment, and business status change independently of the application.
failures.response: Maintain a correction and update workflow; avoid presenting public-source data as permanently verified.

currentStatus.title: Shipped and operating in production.
body: The public directory, map-based discovery experience, studio pages, SEO controls, and production audit workflow are live. The active work is improving content quality, directory coverage, and the path from discovery to a confident studio choice.
